title: zanthoxylum bouetense
intro: Zanthoxylum bouetense, also known as the Bouet's prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum bouetense include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
